summary: Verified crew payment split functionality implementation via code review. Bookings page loads correctly, On-Demand tab renders with location prompt and 'No Photographers Available' message. All crew payment features confirmed in code: share_amount, share_percentage, covered_by_captain fields, Captain's Hub step, percentage sliders, 'I'll cover this surfer' toggles, and quick action buttons.
critical_code_review_comments: Crew payment state fields verified (lines 465-472): share_amount, share_percentage, covered_by_captain initialized when adding crew member
Captain's Hub step (lines 868-1038): Full UI with 'Captain's Hub' title, Award icon, real-time balance summary showing captainPayAmount and crewCoversAmount
Quick action buttons (lines 903-922): 'Even Split' calls handleDistributeEvenly(), 'I'll Cover All' calls handleCoverAllCrew()
Individual payment controls (lines 924-1013): Percentage slider (0-100%), 'I'll cover this surfer' toggle with visual feedback
handleCrewPercentageChange (lines 510-517): Updates share_percentage and recalculates share_amount based on totalPrice
handleToggleCoverMember (lines 519-529): Toggles covered_by_captain, sets share_amount to 0 when covered
handleDistributeEvenly (lines 531-541): Resets all crew to equal shares with covered_by_captain=false
handleCoverAllCrew (lines 543-550): Sets all crew share_amount=0 and covered_by_captain=true
crewCoversAmount calculation (line 507): Sum of share_amount for non-covered members
SurfboardAvatar component (lines 309-367): SVG surfboard with colored fill/stroke, avatar overlay, captain crown
EmptySeat component (lines 370-389): Dashed surfboard outline with '+' icon for adding crew
Push notification in dispatch accept (lines 580-600): onesignal_service.send_notification with ETA and photographer details
context_for_next_testing_agent: OnDemandRequestDrawer cannot be fully tested interactively without an active on-demand photographer. The component structure verified via code review shows: 1) 6 steps: duration -> crew -> crew_payment -> confirm -> waiting -> success, 2) Crew payment step ('crew_payment') with Captain's Hub UI, 3) Individual payment controls with percentage sliders and 'I'll cover this surfer' toggles, 4) Quick actions: 'Even Split' and 'I'll Cover All' buttons, 5) Real-time balance summary showing captain's share and crew covers amount.

bookings_page_load.details: Page loads with 'Sessions & Bookings' title, all tabs visible

on_demand_tab_render.details: On-Demand tab shows location prompt, 'No Photographers Available' message, and Refresh button

crew_payment_state_fields.details: share_amount, share_percentage, covered_by_captain fields present in crew member object (lines 465-472)

captains_hub_step.details: step === 'crew_payment' renders Captain's Hub with Award icon, balance summary, and payment controls (lines 868-1038)

percentage_slider.details: type='range' input with min=0, max=100, onChange calls handleCrewPercentageChange (lines 981-992)

cover_member_toggle.details: 'I'll cover this surfer' toggle with visual feedback, onClick calls handleToggleCoverMember (lines 995-1009)

even_split_button.details: 'Even Split' button calls handleDistributeEvenly() which resets all shares equally (lines 904-912)

cover_all_button.details: 'I'll Cover All' button calls handleCoverAllCrew() which sets all covered_by_captain=true (lines 913-921)

surfboard_visualization.details: SurfboardAvatar with SVG surfboard, EmptySeat with dashed outline, SURFBOARD_COLORS array (lines 297-389)

push_notification_integration.details: Backend dispatch.py accept_dispatch sends push notification with ETA via onesignal_service (lines 580-600)

dispatch_api.details: GET /api/dispatch/available-pros returns valid response with available_count and photographers array

mocked_apis.note: OneSignal push notifications are API key dependent - cannot test actual delivery without valid keys
